# Python Detectors

<!-- Auto-generated by make generate. Do not edit manually. -->

Bomly uses these detector chains when it finds `python` package-manager evidence.

| Package manager | Detector chain | Evidence patterns | Install-first support | Native command hints |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `pdm` | `syft-detector` | `pdm.lock`, `pyproject.toml` | No | `syft for bomly-lite` |
| `pip` | `pip-detector`, `syft-detector` | `requirements.txt`, `requirements-dev.txt`, `requirements.in`, `requirements.lock`, `*requirements*.txt` | No | `pip`, `syft for bomly-lite` |
| `pipenv` | `pipenv-detector`, `syft-detector` | `Pipfile`, `Pipfile.lock` | No | `pipenv`, `syft for bomly-lite` |
| `poetry` | `poetry-detector`, `syft-detector` | `poetry.lock`, `pyproject.toml` | No | `poetry`, `syft for bomly-lite` |
| `setuppy` | `syft-detector` | `setup.py` | No | `syft for bomly-lite` |
| `uv` | `uv-detector`, `syft-detector` | `uv.lock`, `pyproject.toml` | No | `uv`, `syft for bomly-lite` |

## How To Read This

- Bomly tries detector chains from left to right.
- Evidence patterns are files or paths Bomly uses during planning.
- Install-first support means `--install-first` can run the ecosystem's normal install command before graph resolution.
- Syft-backed entries provide broad compatibility, especially for containers and ecosystems without native Bomly graph resolution.
